entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: 4.2-litre conversion variant using overbored cylinders to 98mm, increasing displacement for enhanced performance.
compatibleVariants: DB5 Vantage Saloon, DB5 Vantage Convertible
DB5 Saloon (4.2 conversion), DB5 Convertible (4.2 conversion)
commonFailures: Weber carburettor tuning complexity - specialist synchronisation required for optimal performance
Higher-lift camshaft valvetrain wear - increased stress on tappets and guides
Cooling system overheating under load - original radiator capacity marginal for Vantage output
Cylinder block corrosion between bores - common on original castings, requires specialist inspection
Piston ring wear on overbored cylinders - precise machining critical to prevent oil consumption
Cylinder head gasket failure - increased compression stresses original gasket design

SU carburettor needle wear - affects fuel mixture and performance consistency

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DB5 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DB5's 4.0-litre straight-six is a robust design, but age and mileage bring specific challenges. The 400-series engine family (standard and Vantage) dominates replacement demand, with oil leaks, timing chain wear, and carburettor tuning issues most commonly driving enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Valve Cover Gasket Oil Leaks - Why Aston Martin DB5 Engines Lose Oil Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB5 Saloon, Convertible, Vantage (1963-1965, 400-series engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Original rubber gaskets harden with age and heat cycling, losing sealing ability around valve cover perimeter.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, clean sealing surfaces, fit new gaskets and seals
repairOptions.longevity: 15,000-25,000 miles if quality gaskets used; suitable for early-stage leaks

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine strip, new gaskets throughout, upgraded seals, dyno testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; addresses all wear items simultaneously; recommended for high-mileage engines

problems.recommendation: For isolated valve cover leaks on otherwise sound DB5 engines, specialist gasket replacement offers excellent value. However, if your engine exceeds 40k miles since last major service, consider a full rebuild to address multiple wear points proactively.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 400-series engine prices for your Aston Martin DB5

problems.h4: Timing Chain Wear & Failure - Why Aston Martin DB5 Engines Need Preventative Attention
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB5 all variants (1963-1965, all 400-series engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Original timing chain tensioners wear, allowing chain stretch; guides degrade, risking chain jump and catastrophic valve/piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front cover, replace timing chain, tensioners, guides; reset timing; test run
repairOptions.longevity: 30,000-50,000 miles with quality components; suitable if block/head condition verified good

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine rebuild with upgraded timing components, new bearings, seals, dyno testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; eliminates all timing-related risks; recommended for engines with unknown service history

problems.recommendation: Timing chain work on the DB5 is labour-intensive due to front cover removal. If your engine is otherwise healthy and has documented service history, intermediate replacement offers good value. For engines with uncertain history or high mileage, a full rebuild prevents cascading failures.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB5 timing chain replacement or full engine rebuild

problems.h4: Carburettor Tuning & Performance Issues - Why Aston Martin DB5 Engines Run Poorly
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB5 Saloon (SU HD8 carburettors), DB5 Vantage (Weber 45 DCOE carburettors) (1963-1965)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Performance degradation can occur at any mileage if tuning neglected
problems.rootCause: SU needles wear or stick; Weber jets clog or synchronisation drifts; air/fuel mixture becomes incorrect, causing rough idle, poor throttle response, or black smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, strip, clean, rebuild carburettors; replace needles/jets; synchronise on rolling road
repairOptions.longevity: 10,000-20,000 miles with quality rebuild; suitable for engines with good compression and no internal wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine rebuild with carburettor overhaul or replacement, upgraded components, dyno testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; addresses internal wear that may contribute to poor running; recommended for high-mileage or neglected engines

problems.recommendation: Carburettor tuning is a specialist skill, particularly for Weber-equipped Vantage models. Start with a professional service and tune - many performance issues resolve without engine removal. If compression is low or internal wear is suspected, a full rebuild becomes the more economical long-term choice.
problems.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 carburettor service vs full engine rebuild quotes

problems.h4: Cylinder Block Corrosion - Why Aston Martin DB5 Engines Require Replacement Blocks
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB5 all variants (1963-1965, original 400-series castings)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Corrosion can develop at any age; often discovered during rebuild or compression testing
problems.rootCause: Original cast iron blocks susceptible to internal corrosion between cylinder bores, particularly if coolant maintenance neglected; can cause compression loss, coolant/oil mixing, or catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Block
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Source new/remanufactured block (e.g., Aston Workshop), transfer/rebuild cylinder head, pistons, crank; dyno test; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; eliminates corrosion risk; essential if original block compromised; maintains matching-numbers potential with restamping

problems.recommendation: Block corrosion is not repairable on original DB5 castings. If corrosion is confirmed, a new/remanufactured block is the only viable path. Work with a specialist who can restamp your original engine number for matching-numbers preservation. This is a major investment but protects your DB5's provenance value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB5 replacement block or complete rebuilt engine

problems.h4: Cooling System Overheating - Why Aston Martin DB5 Engines Run Hot Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB5 Vantage (higher output), all variants in hot climates or heavy traffic use (1963-1965)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Overheating can occur at any mileage if cooling system components aged or inadequate
problems.rootCause: Original radiator capacity marginal for Vantage power output; aged hoses, thermostats, or water pumps reduce efficiency; inadequate airflow in traffic exacerbates heat buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator with high-capacity aluminium unit, new hoses, thermostat, water pump; flush system; test under load
repairOptions.longevity: Significant improvement in cooling capacity; suitable for engines in good mechanical condition; recommended for Vantage models or hot-climate use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine rebuild with upgraded cooling components integrated, new gaskets/seals, dyno testing with thermal monitoring, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; addresses internal wear that may be exacerbated by overheating; recommended if overheating has caused damage

problems.recommendation: Start with a comprehensive cooling system upgrade before considering engine replacement. Many overheating issues resolve with a high-capacity radiator and fresh components. If overheating has already caused head gasket failure or warping, a full rebuild becomes necessary.
problems.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 cooling system upgrade vs engine rebuild costs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DB5
finalCta.paragraph: The DB5's 400-series engine is a masterpiece of 1960s engineering, but age demands proactive care. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand matching-numbers preservation, Vantage specification, and the unique challenges of classic Aston Martin rebuilds. Every quoted engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from trusted specialists today and protect your DB5's value without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DB5 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DB5 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin DB5 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Aston Martin DB5 is listed below, with compatible 400-series engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help identify your replacement options.

groups.title: Aston Martin DB5 Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin DB5 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The DB5's original 4.0-litre (3,995cc) straight-six, designed by Tadek Marek, powered all 1963-1965 production in standard 282bhp SU-carburettor or 314bhp Vantage Weber-carburettor specification. This iconic engine defines the DB5 character, with robust construction but age-related wear requiring specialist attention during replacement or rebuild.
commonFailurePoints: cooling system marginality on Vantage models
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DB5 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2-litre DB5 conversion increases displacement to 4,200cc via 98mm bore (from standard 96mm), delivering 310-325bhp while retaining the original 400-series block architecture. Popular among owners seeking enhanced performance without the complexity of larger conversions, this option requires precise machining and specialist component selection.
commonFailurePoints: piston ring wear on overbored cylinders
head gasket stress from increased compression
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DB5 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.7-litre DB5 competition conversion represents the ultimate performance upgrade, delivering 340bhp+ through significant block modifications, bespoke pistons and racing-specification components. Reserved for track-focused or high-performance road use, this option demands expert engineering and comprehensive supporting modifications.
commonFailurePoints: cooling system demands at elevated power levels
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 4.7L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Aston Martin DB5 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from standard 4.0-litre SU to Vantage Weber specification.

items.title: Aston Martin DB5 Petrol Engines
items.description: All Aston Martin DB5 variants were powered exclusively by petrol-fuelled 400-series straight-six engines, with displacement options of 4.0L (standard), 4.2L (conversion) or 4.7L (competition).
items.descriptor: All Aston Martin DB5 variants were powered exclusively by petrol-fuelled 400-series straight-six engines, with displacement options of 4.0L (standard), 4.2L (conversion) or 4.7L (competition).
items.familiesLabel: Common Aston Martin DB5 Petrol Engine Families
families: 400/1461 - Standard 4.0L SU-carburettor engine (282bhp), most common replacement candidate
400/2025/V - Vantage 4.0L Weber-carburettor engine (314bhp), highest demand for specialist rebuilds
400/1306 - 4.2L conversion engine (310-325bhp), popular performance upgrade option
knownFor: Robust Tadek Marek straight-six design with proven longevity when maintained
Valve cover oil leaks and timing chain wear as most common age-related failures
Vantage Weber carburettors require specialist tuning expertise for optimal performance
Matching-numbers preservation critical for collector value; non-original engines reduce desirability
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB5 Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin DB5 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the DB5's 1963-1965 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their 400-series engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: First-year DB5 production introduced the 4.0-litre 400-series straight-six with SU carburettors, establishing the foundation for all subsequent variants.
keyChanges: Introduction of Tadek Marek 4.0L straight-six engine (3,995cc, 96mm bore, 92mm stroke)
Standard specification: triple SU HD8 carburettors, 282bhp at 5,500rpm, 8.7:1 compression
ZF 5-speed manual gearbox standard; BorgWarner Type 35 automatic optional
Early cylinder head castings unique to 1963 production; verify bonnet clearance compatibility
knownFor: Earliest DB5 engines command premium for matching-numbers restoration projects
Early cylinder head castings may require verification for correct carburettor fitment
Limited production numbers increase rarity value for original-engine examples
Specialist knowledge required for authentic period-correct rebuild specification
years.cta: Get quotes for 1963 Aston Martin DB5 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB5 1963 engine rebuild cost • DB5 early production engine number verification • DB5 matching numbers engine preservation
enquiries: DB5 early production engine number verification

years.description: 1964 saw refinement of the 4.0L engine and the introduction of the coveted Vantage specification with triple Weber carburettors and higher-lift camshafts.
keyChanges: Introduction of DB5 Vantage specification: triple Weber 45 DCOE carburettors, higher-lift inlet camshafts, 314bhp at 5,750rpm
Engine number suffix "/V" introduced to denote factory Vantage specification
Refined camshaft profiles and cylinder head casting improvements for mid-production engines
4.2-litre conversion option becomes available through specialist workshops
knownFor: Highest concentration of factory Vantage engines produced; "/V" suffix critical for provenance
Mid-production engines benefit from refined components but still susceptible to age-related wear
Vantage Weber carburettors require specialist tuning expertise; poor setup causes performance issues
Matching-numbers Vantage engines command significant premium in collector market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1964 Aston Martin DB5 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB5 Vantage engine rebuild cost • DB5 Weber carburettor tuning specialist • DB5 matching numbers Vantage engine value
enquiries: DB5 matching numbers Vantage engine value

years.description: Final-year DB5 production saw the highest concentration of Vantage specification engines and the transition toward DB6 architecture, with late engines carrying over key characteristics.
keyChanges: Highest concentration of factory Vantage engines produced; "/V" suffix engines most sought-after
Late-production cylinder head and block casting refinements for improved reliability
Some late 1965 engines carry over to early DB6 but with different engine mount positions
4.2-litre and 4.7-litre conversion options gain popularity among performance-oriented owners
mainEngines: 400/2034 - 4.0L petrol (among last DB5 engines before DB6 introduction)
popularModels: Aston Martin DB5 Vantage Saloon (Late Production)
knownFor: Late-production engines benefit from accumulated manufacturing refinements but still require specialist rebuild expertise
Vantage Convertible engines exceptionally rare; matching-numbers preservation critical for value
Transition to DB6 architecture means late DB5 engines not directly interchangeable with DB6 without modification
Final-year DB5 engines highly desirable for authentic period-correct restorations
years.cta: Get quotes for 1965 Aston Martin DB5 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB5 1965 engine rebuild cost • DB5 Vantage Convertible engine specialist • DB5 to DB6 engine compatibility check
enquiries: DB5 to DB6 engine compatibility check

faq.h2: Aston Martin DB5 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin DB5 engine rebuild cost in the UK?
items.answer: DB5 engine rebuild costs typically range from £18,000 for standard specification to £60,000+ for Vantage or 4.2-litre conversions, depending on originality requirements and component upgrades.
keyPoints: Standard 4.0L SU-carburettor rebuilds start around £18,000-£35,000 supply-only
Vantage Weber-specification rebuilds command £25,000-£60,000 due to carburettor complexity
4.2-litre or 4.7-litre conversions add £5,000-£15,000 to base rebuild costs
Matching-numbers preservation may increase costs but protects collector value significantly
items.cta: Compare rebuilt DB5 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Does matching-numbers affect my Aston Martin DB5's value?
items.answer: Yes - matching-numbers typically adds 15-25% to DB5 value, while non-original engines can reduce collector desirability and resale value by up to 50%.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers means chassis, engine, gearbox and differential all match factory build records
Non-matching engines significantly impact concours eligibility and investment-grade status
Specialist restamping services can preserve provenance when original block is compromised
Always verify engine number against Aston Martin Heritage Trust records before purchase
items.cta: Verify your DB5 matching-numbers status with a specialist

items.question: What are the most common Aston Martin DB5 engine problems?
items.answer: Valve cover oil leaks, timing chain wear, and carburettor tuning issues dominate DB5 engine failure enquiries, with block corrosion a critical concern for original castings.
keyPoints: Valve cover gasket leaks common after 30k miles; repair £700-£1,500 specialist
Timing chain tensioner wear documented at 40k-60k miles; replacement £5,000-£12,000
SU/Weber carburettor tuning requires specialist expertise; poor setup causes performance loss
Cylinder block corrosion between bores may require new aftermarket block (£15,000-£25,000)
items.cta: Get quotes for DB5 engine problem diagnosis and repair

items.question: Should I rebuild my original DB5 engine or buy a replacement?
items.answer: Rebuilding preserves matching-numbers provenance critical for value, while replacement engines offer faster turnaround but may impact collector desirability if not properly documented.
keyPoints: Rebuild your original block if corrosion-free to maintain matching-numbers status
Replacement engines from Aston Workshop offer visually identical new blocks with internal improvements
Exchange engine schemes can reduce cost by £5,000-£10,000 but forfeit original block
Always declare restamped engine numbers for transparency and legal compliance
items.cta: Discuss rebuild vs replacement options with a DB5 specialist

items.question: How long does an Aston Martin DB5 engine rebuild take?
items.answer: DB5 engine rebuilds typically take 4-8 months for crate-engine supply, or 12-18 months for full restoration including body-off work and matching-numbers documentation.
keyPoints: Crate rebuilt engines (supply-only) available in 4-8 months from specialist workshops
Full restoration including body-off, paint and trim extends timeline to 12-18 months
Vantage specification or 4.2-litre conversions may add 2-4 weeks for carburettor tuning
Lead times vary by specialist workload; book consultations early for planning
items.cta: Check current DB5 engine rebuild lead times from UK specialists

items.question: Can I upgrade my DB5 to Vantage specification?
items.answer: Yes - DB5 Vantage upgrades (Weber carburettors + higher-lift camshafts) cost £12,000-£18,000 plus base rebuild, but require specialist expertise and may impact matching-numbers status.
keyPoints: Vantage conversion replaces SU carburettors with triple Weber 45 DCOE units
Higher-lift inlet camshafts and revised ignition timing required for 314bhp output
Conversion must be documented for provenance; affects matching-numbers classification
Specialist tuning essential; poor setup causes performance issues and increased wear
items.cta: Get quotes for DB5 Vantage specification upgrade

items.question: Is a 4.2-litre conversion reliable for my Aston Martin DB5?
items.answer: Yes - DB5 4.2-litre conversions are reliable with correct machining and components, using the original block overbored to 98mm with forged pistons and appropriate compression ratio.
keyPoints: Conversion requires precision boring to 98mm (from standard 96mm) with forged pistons
Compression ratio typically set to 9.5:1 for balanced performance and reliability
Cylinder head gasket and cooling system upgrades recommended for increased output
Specialist engineering essential; improper machining risks block integrity and longevity
items.cta: Compare DB5 4.2-litre conversion specialists and pricing

items.question: What warranty do rebuilt Aston Martin DB5 engines carry?
items.answer: Rebuilt DB5 engines from accredited UK specialists typically carry 12-24 month or 12,000-mile warranties on parts and labour, with terms varying by workshop and specification.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard through EngineMarket network
Premium rebuilds (Vantage, 4.2-litre) may include extended 24-month coverage options
Warranty terms should specify coverage for upgraded components addressing original failures
Always confirm exact warranty scope, exclusions and claim process before commitment
items.cta: Compare DB5 engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: Which UK specialists are best for Aston Martin DB5 engine rebuilds?
items.answer: Leading DB5 engine specialists include RS Williams, Aston Workshop, and Nicholas Mee - each with proven expertise in matching-numbers preservation and Vantage specification.
keyPoints: RS Williams: Extensive DB5 rebuild experience, heritage certification support
Aston Workshop: New block production, visually identical to original castings
Nicholas Mee: Concours-level rebuilds, matching-numbers documentation expertise
Compare quotes, lead times and warranty terms before selecting a specialist
items.cta: Get quotes from multiple DB5 engine specialists for comparison

items.question: Will any DB5 engine fit my car regardless of year?
items.answer: All DB5 400-series engines physically interchange across 1963-1965 production, but early cars require correct cylinder head for bonnet clearance and emissions configurations vary by market.
keyPoints: 400-series engines share identical mounting points and external dimensions
Early 1963 cylinder heads may require verification for bonnet clearance on late chassis
US-market cars need different throttle linkage and emissions configurations
Vantage engines require compatible carburettor linkages and cooling capacity
items.cta: Verify engine compatibility for your specific DB5 chassis and market
